summary: A single functional test failed because pip could not download a test package from GitHub due to repeated HTTP 502 errors (network/server error).
root_cause: The test tests/functional/test_truststore.py::test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub] failed because pip could not fetch https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip; the run produced an OSError from an HTTPSConnectionPool after repeated 502 error responses.

evidence.reason: Identifies the failing test that exercised a GitHub URL.
Shows the test attempted to download a package directly from GitHub.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: Concrete error message indicating the download failed due to repeated 502 responses from GitHub, causing an OSError.
The test harness reports the pip invocation exited with a non-zero return code, causing the test to fail.

suggested_fix: This appears to be a transient network/server error. Retry the CI run first. To make tests more robust, avoid relying on GitHub being reachable in functional tests—serve the test package from a local test server or fixture/mirror, or add retry/backoff logic in the test when downloading external URLs.
